channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: BIGS-DR Scholarships 2026 at ZEF, University of Bonn: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity for Global South Researchers in Sustainable Development
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Center for Development Research (ZEF) at the University of Bonn is inviting applications for the prestigious BIGS-DR Scholarships 2026. This fully funded doctoral opportunity is designed for talented young researchers from the Global South who are passionate about sustainable development and interdisciplinary research. Through its structured doctoral program, the Bonn International Graduate School for […]

The post BIGS-DR Scholarships 2026 at ZEF, University of Bonn: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity for Global South Researchers in Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Center for Development Research (ZEF) at the University of Bonn is inviting applications for the prestigious BIGS-DR Scholarships 2026. This fully funded doctoral opportunity is designed for talented young researchers from the Global South who are passionate about sustainable development and interdisciplinary research.

Through its structured doctoral program, the Bonn International Graduate School for Development Research (BIGS-DR), ZEF provides an exceptional academic environment for scholars aiming to address some of the world’s most pressing global challenges.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the BIGS-DR Scholarships is August 31, 2026.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare their research proposals and supporting documents well in advance to ensure a competitive application.

About the Programme

The BIGS-DR doctoral programme is an interdisciplinary PhD track that combines academic excellence with practical relevance. It supports students throughout their doctoral journey, from initial coursework to final dissertation.

Participants benefit from:

  • Structured doctoral training modules
  • Interdisciplinary research approaches
  • Soft skills and career development training
  • Close supervision by international experts

ZEF hosts approximately 50 senior researchers from diverse disciplines, providing a rich intellectual environment where doctoral candidates can thrive and develop innovative research solutions.

Research Focus Areas

Applicants are expected to submit a creative and impactful research proposal aligned with sustainable development themes. Relevant topics include:

  • Sustainable food systems
  • Agriculture and rural development
  • Nutrition and public health
  • Climate change and environmental sustainability
  • Biodiversity conservation
  • Migration and development
  • Governance and institutional frameworks
  • Conflict and development dynamics

The programme strongly encourages interdisciplinary approaches that connect multiple fields to address complex global challenges.

Funding and Scholarships

The BIGS-DR programme offers EPOS scholarships, funded by the DAAD (Deutscher Akademischer Austauschdienst).

These scholarships typically cover:

  • Monthly stipend
  • Travel costs
  • Health insurance
  • Academic and research support

The funding aims to enable scholars to focus fully on their research while gaining international academic exposure.

Academic Environment and Benefits

ZEF is recognized as a leading global institute for development research. As part of the programme, doctoral candidates will:

  • Join a diverse, international cohort of researchers
  • Engage in collaborative and interdisciplinary learning
  • Access world-class academic supervision
  • Participate in workshops, seminars, and global research networks

This environment fosters both academic excellence and professional growth, preparing scholars for careers in academia, international organizations, policy-making, and development practice.

Why Choose BIGS-DR at ZEF

The BIGS-DR programme stands out for its strong integration of research, policy, and practice. It equips scholars with the tools needed to generate impactful knowledge and contribute to sustainable development solutions worldwide.

By studying at one of Germany’s top universities and engaging with a global network of researchers, participants gain both academic credibility and practical expertise.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities click here 

The post BIGS-DR Scholarships 2026 at ZEF, University of Bonn: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity for Global South Researchers in Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme 2026: Paid Summer Sustainability Opportunity for Undergraduate Students
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme (FLP) 2026 is now open for applications, offering a unique paid summer opportunity for current and recent undergraduate students passionate about climate and sustainability. This prestigious programme provides hands-on experience through real-world climate projects, professional development training, and networking opportunities with experts across academia, policy, and industry. Application Deadline […]

The post Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme 2026: Paid Summer Sustainability Opportunity for Undergraduate Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme (FLP) 2026 is now open for applications, offering a unique paid summer opportunity for current and recent undergraduate students passionate about climate and sustainability.

This prestigious programme provides hands-on experience through real-world climate projects, professional development training, and networking opportunities with experts across academia, policy, and industry.

Application Deadline and Programme Dates

Applications must be submitted by Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 11:59 PM (UK time).

The programme will run from:

  • Start Date: Monday, July 13, 2026
  • End Date: Friday, September 11, 2026

Participants are entitled to 5 days of annual leave during this period.

Programme Structure and Work Experience

Participants, known as Project Assistants, will be assigned to specific climate and sustainability projects hosted by Cambridge Zero and its partners.

Projects span a wide range of areas, including:

  • Climate policy and adaptation
  • Sustainability research
  • Climate education
  • Innovation and mapping initiatives
  • Environmental health and future housing

Project Assistants will:

  • Work collaboratively in professional environments
  • Engage with researchers, policymakers, and stakeholders
  • Contribute to meaningful outputs such as policy briefings, research reports, and communication materials

For example, in the Climate Adaptation Policy project, participants will:

  • Develop a policy briefing based on national climate reports
  • Conduct research and stakeholder consultations
  • Potentially contribute to government evidence submissions

Professional Development and Career Growth

Beyond project work, the Future Leaders Programme includes a structured development component designed to support participants’ long-term careers.

Participants will:

  • Explore diverse career pathways in climate and sustainability
  • Reflect on personal strengths and professional goals
  • Receive coaching and mentorship from experts
  • Build a strong peer network within their cohort

This combination of practical experience and personal development ensures participants gain valuable skills applicable to future academic pursuits and job opportunities.

Key Benefits of the Programme

  • Paid summer work experience in climate and sustainability
  • Opportunity to work on real-world, impactful projects
  • Direct engagement with professionals and experts
  • Structured career development and coaching
  • Access to a network of like-minded peers and alumni

Many past participants have highlighted the programme’s role in shaping their careers, helping them build both technical expertise and professional confidence.

Salary, Location, and Working Conditions

  • Salary: £13.45 per hour (aligned with the UK real Living Wage)
  • Working Hours: 37 hours per week
  • Location: In-person in Cambridge, United Kingdom
  • Flexibility: Some flexible working arrangements may be considered

Participants must be able to work in Cambridge for the duration of the programme.

Eligibility Criteria

The programme is open to:

  • Current undergraduate students
  • Recent graduates

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Interest in climate and sustainability
  • Strong motivation to learn and contribute
  • Ability to work collaboratively in a professional setting

Why You Should Apply

The Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme stands out as a comprehensive early-career opportunity in the climate field. It not only provides paid work experience but also equips participants with the knowledge, skills, and networks needed to succeed in sustainability careers.

By combining hands-on project work with structured professional development, the programme prepares the next generation of climate leaders to tackle global environmental challenges effectively.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme 2026: Paid Summer Sustainability Opportunity for Undergraduate Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GAIN Mentor Program 2026/2027: Volunteer to Guide African Students into Graduate School (Global Opportunity)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Graduate Application International Network (GAIN) Mentor Program 2026/2027 is a unique global volunteer opportunity designed for experienced academics and professionals who are passionate about increasing African representation in graduate education. This impactful mentorship initiative connects mentors with aspiring African students, guiding them through the complex graduate application process in fields such as economics, public […]

The post GAIN Mentor Program 2026/2027: Volunteer to Guide African Students into Graduate School (Global Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Graduate Application International Network (GAIN) Mentor Program 2026/2027 is a unique global volunteer opportunity designed for experienced academics and professionals who are passionate about increasing African representation in graduate education. This impactful mentorship initiative connects mentors with aspiring African students, guiding them through the complex graduate application process in fields such as economics, public policy, political science, and public health.

By becoming a GAIN mentor, you will play a direct role in shaping the academic and professional futures of talented African students while contributing to a broader mission of equity and inclusion in global academia.

About the GAIN Program

The GAIN program is a structured, multi-phase initiative that supports African students throughout their graduate application journey. It combines educational webinars, personalized mentorship, and financial support to reduce barriers to higher education.

The program is divided into three main components:

  1. Webinar Program
  2. Mentoring Program
  3. Alumni Support Network

Each component is designed to progressively equip participants with the knowledge, resources, and confidence needed to successfully apply to graduate schools worldwide.

The Role of a GAIN Mentor

GAIN mentors are at the heart of the program. They provide personalized guidance and support to mentees navigating the graduate application process.

Core Responsibilities

Mentors typically:

  • Guide one mentee through the entire application cycle
  • Conduct regular virtual meetings (usually bi-weekly)
  • Provide feedback on application materials such as CVs and statements of purpose
  • Offer advice on selecting universities and programs
  • Support preparation for standardized tests such as GRE and TOEFL
  • Share personal experiences and insights from their academic journeys

In addition to one-on-one mentoring, some mentors may choose to:

  • Support group sessions or workshops
  • Offer expertise in specific areas such as scholarship applications or essay editing
  • Participate in collaborative mentor networks or peer-support groups

Time Commitment and Duration

Mentors are expected to commit a moderate but consistent amount of time throughout the program.

Key Details

  • Commitment: Approximately 4–8 hours per month
  • Duration: May/June 2026 – April 2027
  • Format: Fully virtual engagement

This flexible structure allows mentors to balance their professional responsibilities while making a meaningful impact.

Who Typically Becomes a GAIN Mentor

GAIN mentors are usually individuals who have successfully navigated graduate education themselves and are willing to support others on the same path.

Typical Mentor Profiles

  • PhD students
  • Academic researchers
  • University professors
  • Policy professionals and advisors

Most mentors have firsthand experience with graduate applications and understand the challenges involved.

Eligibility Requirements for Mentors

To register as a GAIN mentor, applicants must meet the following criteria:

Required Criteria

  • Ability to commit 4–8 hours per month throughout the mentoring cycle
  • Experience completing a graduate application process in:
    • Economics
    • Public policy
    • Political science
    • Public health
    • Or related disciplines
  • Strong interest in increasing African representation in academia
  • Willingness to guide and support mentees consistently

Preferred Criteria

  • Previous mentoring experience
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills
  • Commitment to diversity, inclusion, and academic equity

Overview of the GAIN Program Structure

Understanding the full GAIN program helps mentors better support their mentees.

1. Webinar Program (January – June)

This foundational phase provides participants with essential knowledge about the graduate application process.

Topics covered include:

  • Scholarships and funding opportunities
  • GRE, TOEFL, and standardized test strategies
  • Writing academic CVs and statements of purpose
  • Securing letters of recommendation
  • Choosing universities and career paths
  • Balancing academic pursuits with personal responsibilities

Webinars are held every two weeks and feature leading African scholars and experts.

2. Mentoring Program

This is the core phase where mentors actively engage with mentees.

Key features include:

  • One-on-one mentor-mentee matching based on:
    • Field of study
    • Professional background
    • Country of origin
  • Bi-weekly meetings and continuous support
  • Guidance on application preparation and submission

The mentoring phase begins with a GRE summer bootcamp, providing participants with access to preparation materials and structured support.

3. Financial Support for Mentees

GAIN provides financial assistance to selected participants to reduce barriers to application.

Support includes:

  • Application fee coverage
  • Standardized test fees (GRE, TOEFL)
  • Score submission costs

High-performing mentees may qualify for Mentoring+, which offers:

  • Increased financial support
  • Enhanced mentorship engagement

4. GAIN Alumni Network

After successful admission into graduate programs, mentees transition into the GAIN Alumni Network.

Benefits include:

  • Monthly check-in sessions
  • Continued mentorship and guidance
  • Targeted webinars and academic support
  • Community building and networking opportunities

This ensures long-term success beyond admission.

Why You Should Become a GAIN Mentor

Becoming a GAIN mentor is not only an opportunity to give back but also a chance to grow professionally and personally.

Key Benefits

  • Contribute to increasing African representation in global academia
  • Support talented students in achieving their academic goals
  • Build meaningful professional relationships
  • Gain mentorship and leadership experience
  • Join a global network of academics and professionals

Application Process

To become a GAIN mentor:

  • Complete the Online Mentor Registration Form
  • Provide details about your academic and professional background
  • Indicate your availability and areas of expertise

Application Deadline

The deadline to register as a GAIN Mentor for the 2026/2027 cycle is:

April 30, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early to secure placement in the program.

Click HERE to learn more about the GAIN Mentor Program 2026/2027.

Discover more global Mentorship and African opportunities on OFY.

The post GAIN Mentor Program 2026/2027: Volunteer to Guide African Students into Graduate School (Global Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Researcher/Writer – Climate Adaptation & Resilience : Global Remote Opportunity with the Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB)
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Institute for Human Rights and Business is currently seeking 1–2 experienced Researchers/Writers specializing in climate adaptation and resilience to lead a new phase of its flagship JUST Stories project. This is a unique global opportunity for professionals who combine rigorous research expertise with exceptional storytelling skills and a deep understanding of climate justice. This […]

The post Researcher/Writer – Climate Adaptation & Resilience : Global Remote Opportunity with the Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Institute for Human Rights and Business is currently seeking 1–2 experienced Researchers/Writers specializing in climate adaptation and resilience to lead a new phase of its flagship JUST Stories project. This is a unique global opportunity for professionals who combine rigorous research expertise with exceptional storytelling skills and a deep understanding of climate justice.

This role sits at the intersection of climate action, human rights, and global development, offering the chance to contribute to a transformative initiative aimed at reshaping how climate adaptation is understood, financed, and implemented worldwide.

Application Deadline and Timeline

Applications must be submitted by Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 11:59 PM (UK time).

Shortlisted candidates will be invited to:

  • First-round interviews: April 23–24, 2026
  • Second-round interviews: Scheduled the following week

The expected start date is April or May 2026, with the contract running through September 2027.

About the JUST Stories Project

The JUST Stories initiative, launched in 2024, is a global effort to identify and document real-world examples of effective, people-centered climate action. While the first phase focused on decarbonization, this second phase shifts attention toward climate adaptation and resilience.

The project is grounded in a critical insight: climate solutions fail when they exclude the communities most affected. By contrast, successful adaptation emerges from inclusive, locally grounded, and co-designed approaches.

Over the next 18 months, the project will:

  • Identify and document community-led adaptation solutions worldwide
  • Highlight best practices and enabling conditions
  • Translate these stories into actionable insights for policymakers, investors, and practitioners

These outputs are not merely narratives but serve as evidence-based tools to inform decision-making across finance, policy, and corporate sectors.

Role Overview

The selected Researchers/Writers will play a central role in shaping this next phase by producing immersive, field-based stories that bridge the gap between local realities and global decision-making systems.

The role requires the ability to operate across two critical dimensions:

  • Ground-level engagement with communities and frontline actors
  • System-level understanding of finance, governance, and climate policy

Key Responsibilities

Research and Analysis

  • Conduct in-depth literature reviews on climate adaptation, resilience, and locally-led approaches
  • Identify high-impact case studies across diverse global contexts
  • Develop frameworks to assess effective and equitable adaptation

Fieldwork and Primary Research

  • Design and conduct interviews with community leaders, institutions, and workers
  • Undertake international field visits to gather firsthand data and narratives
  • Ensure ethical research practices, including consent and safeguarding

Narrative Development

  • Produce high-quality, publication-ready stories integrating human experience and analytical insight
  • Translate complex findings into clear, actionable recommendations
  • Collaborate with communications teams to create multi-format outputs (articles, briefings, audio, visual content)

Strategic Contribution

  • Support cross-sector engagement involving policymakers, investors, and civil society
  • Contribute to synthesizing lessons across case studies
  • Participate in global convenings such as Climate Weeks and COP events

Required Expertise and Skills

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • At least 7 years of experience in research and writing
  • Proven expertise in climate adaptation and resilience
  • Strong background in qualitative research and fieldwork
  • Exceptional storytelling and analytical writing abilities
  • Understanding of global frameworks such as UN climate and human rights standards

Experience in working with Indigenous communities, NGOs, or climate finance actors is highly valued.

Contract Details and Compensation

  • Type: Consultancy contract under UK law
  • Duration: April/May 2026 – September 2027
  • Compensation: Approximately £70,000 total budget
  • Structure Options:
    • One full-time lead researcher/writer
    • Two part-time contributors

Candidates must:

  • Have the legal right to work in their country
  • Provide their own equipment and internet access
  • Be available for international travel (up to three times per year)

Work Environment

This is a fully remote role open globally. However, candidates must be located in time zones compatible with Central European Time (CET) or westward to ensure collaboration with teams operating on UK and West Africa time.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This role represents more than a writing or research position. It is an opportunity to directly influence how climate adaptation is conceptualized and implemented globally.

By documenting and amplifying community-led solutions, the selected candidates will help shift dominant narratives away from top-down approaches toward inclusive, effective, and sustainable climate action.

Professionals passionate about climate justice, human rights, and impactful storytelling are strongly encouraged to apply.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here  

The post Researcher/Writer – Climate Adaptation & Resilience : Global Remote Opportunity with the Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants 2026: $2.5 Million Funding Opportunity for Nonprofits Supporting Youth, Storytelling, and Community Engagement
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants 2026 program is now officially open, offering a significant funding opportunity for nonprofit organizations working to strengthen local communities. With a total of $2.5 million in grants available, this initiative reflects NBCUniversal’s commitment to empowering grassroots organizations and fostering sustainable community development. This annual grant program is designed to support […]

The post NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants 2026: $2.5 Million Funding Opportunity for Nonprofits Supporting Youth, Storytelling, and Community Engagement appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants 2026 program is now officially open, offering a significant funding opportunity for nonprofit organizations working to strengthen local communities. With a total of $2.5 million in grants available, this initiative reflects NBCUniversal’s commitment to empowering grassroots organizations and fostering sustainable community development.

This annual grant program is designed to support nonprofit organizations operating in select markets where NBC and Telemundo-owned television stations are present. By providing unrestricted funding, NBCUniversal ensures that recipient organizations have the flexibility to allocate resources where they are most needed, enabling them to maximize their impact.

Application Timeline and Deadline

Applications for the 2026 NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants will be accepted from March 24, 2026, through April 24, 2026.

All applications must be submitted before 7:59:59 PM Eastern Time on April 24, 2026. Late submissions will not be considered under any circumstances, making it essential for applicants to plan and submit their proposals well in advance of the deadline.

Funding Overview

NBCUniversal will award grants to eligible nonprofit organizations with annual operating expenses ranging between $100,000 and $1,000,000. The grants are categorized as general operating funds, meaning recipients are not restricted to specific project expenditures. Instead, funding can support broader organizational needs, including staffing, program expansion, infrastructure, and operational sustainability.

Key Focus Areas

The NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants program prioritizes organizations that are actively working within three major thematic areas:

1. Youth Education and Empowerment

This category supports programs that equip young people with essential tools for success. Initiatives may include:

  • STEM and STEAM education programs
  • Youth entrepreneurship development
  • In-school and community-based educational support
  • Skill-building and leadership development initiatives

Organizations that focus on preparing youth for future academic and professional success are strongly encouraged to apply.

2. Next Generation Storytellers

This category is dedicated to nurturing emerging voices and creative talent. Eligible programs include:

  • Media and communications training
  • Arts, journalism, and storytelling initiatives
  • Opportunities in sports and entertainment industries
  • Platforms that amplify youth voices and perspectives

This focus area aims to diversify and strengthen the pipeline of talent in storytelling and media industries.

3. Community Engagement

Programs under this category aim to strengthen social cohesion and civic participation. Examples include:

  • Volunteerism initiatives
  • Community-building events and programs
  • Projects that encourage local engagement and collaboration
  • Efforts to foster unity and inclusivity within communities

Organizations working to create meaningful connections and engagement at the community level will find strong alignment with this category.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants, applicants must:

  • Be a registered nonprofit organization
  • Operate within one of the eligible NBC or Telemundo station markets
  • Have total annual expenses between $100,000 and $1,000,000
  • Demonstrate measurable impact within their local community
  • Align their work with at least one of the three focus areas

Application Process

Interested organizations must complete their applications through the official NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants portal. Additionally, applicants are encouraged to register for informational webinars hosted by NBCUniversal to gain insights into the application process, evaluation criteria, and best practices for submission.

Applicants should ensure that their proposals clearly articulate their mission, impact, and alignment with NBCUniversal’s priority areas. Strong applications typically highlight measurable outcomes, community relevance, and sustainability.

Why This Grant Matters

The NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants stand out due to their flexibility and emphasis on local impact. By offering unrestricted funding, the program recognizes the importance of empowering nonprofits to address community needs in dynamic and adaptive ways.

Moreover, the focus on youth, storytelling, and community engagement reflects a forward-looking approach to social development—one that values innovation, inclusivity, and grassroots leadership.

Organizations seeking to expand their reach, strengthen their operations, or deepen their community impact should strongly consider applying for this opportunity.

For more information click here

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants 2026: $2.5 Million Funding Opportunity for Nonprofits Supporting Youth, Storytelling, and Community Engagement appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD in African Health Economics & Diagnostics at LSE 2026: Complete Guide to the AHEaD Studentship Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE) is offering two highly competitive, fully funded PhD studentships under the Wellcome-funded African Health, Economics and Diagnostics (AHEaD) Project. This prestigious doctoral opportunity is designed for candidates who are passionate about advancing research at the intersection of health systems, economics, and diagnostic technologies, particularly within African […]

The post Fully Funded PhD in African Health Economics & Diagnostics at LSE 2026: Complete Guide to the AHEaD Studentship Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE) is offering two highly competitive, fully funded PhD studentships under the Wellcome-funded African Health, Economics and Diagnostics (AHEaD) Project. This prestigious doctoral opportunity is designed for candidates who are passionate about advancing research at the intersection of health systems, economics, and diagnostic technologies, particularly within African contexts.

This studentship goes beyond a traditional PhD. It integrates rigorous academic training with real-world policy research, offering candidates the chance to contribute directly to a major international research programme shaping the future of diagnostics and healthcare systems across Africa.

About the AHEaD Project

The African Health, Economics and Diagnostics (AHEaD) Project is a major research initiative funded by Wellcome, focused on understanding and improving the role of diagnostics in health systems across Africa.

Diagnostics, particularly point-of-care tests, are critical in improving early detection, treatment decisions, and overall health outcomes. However, their accessibility, affordability, and effectiveness are influenced by complex economic, political, and systemic factors.

The AHEaD project seeks to address these challenges by examining:

  • How diagnostic tools are developed, produced, and distributed
  • The economic and health impacts of diagnostic technologies
  • Policy frameworks that influence diagnostic markets
  • Barriers to access and adoption across different health systems

PhD candidates will play a central role in this research, contributing to evidence-based insights that can influence policy, markets, and healthcare delivery systems.

Programme Overview

Successful applicants will be enrolled in the MPhil/PhD in Health Policy at LSE while working closely with the AHEaD research team.

Key Programme Details

  • Institution: London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE)
  • Department: LSE Health & Department of Health Policy
  • Programme: MPhil/PhD in Health Policy
  • Start Date: September 2026
  • Duration: 4 years (full-time)
  • Funding: Fully funded (home and overseas tuition covered)
  • Deadline: April 27, 2026 (5:00 PM)

This dual structure ensures that candidates receive both world-class academic training and hands-on research experience.

Research Scope and Thematic Areas

The AHEaD PhD studentships support a wide range of research topics within the economics of diagnostics. Candidates are encouraged to develop innovative, interdisciplinary research proposals aligned with the project’s goals.

Key Research Areas

  1. Political Economy of Diagnostics
    • Manufacturing systems and supply chains
    • Market dynamics and governance structures
    • Institutional influences on diagnostic availability
  2. Health and Economic Impact
    • Cost-effectiveness of diagnostic technologies
    • Macroeconomic benefits of local production
    • Impact on healthcare delivery and outcomes
  3. Health Technology Assessment (HTA)
    • Evaluation of diagnostic tools
    • Reimbursement and pricing models
    • Policy implications for adoption and scale-up
  4. Financing and Regulatory Frameworks
    • Investment strategies in diagnostics
    • Regulatory policies and incentives
    • Sustainable market development
  5. Access and Uptake of Diagnostics
    • Affordability challenges
    • Behavioral and systemic barriers
    • Data integration and digital health systems

While the research has a strong African focus, applicants are encouraged to incorporate comparative or global perspectives where relevant.

Structure of the PhD Programme

The four-year doctoral programme is carefully structured to balance coursework, independent research, and active participation in the AHEaD project.

Year 1: Foundation and Research Development

  • Completion of required coursework in health policy and economics
  • Development and refinement of PhD research topic
  • Early engagement with AHEaD research activities

Years 2–4: Advanced Research and Fieldwork

  • Full-time doctoral research
  • Fieldwork and primary data collection (including international travel)
  • Collaboration with global research partners
  • Contribution to AHEaD project outputs

Ongoing Academic Engagement

Throughout the programme, candidates will:

  • Meet regularly with supervisors and project managers
  • Participate in seminars, workshops, and academic discussions
  • Contribute to collaborative research initiatives
  • Engage in interdisciplinary learning and networking

Funding and Financial Support

One of the most attractive aspects of this opportunity is its comprehensive financial package.

What the Funding Covers

  • Full tuition fees for both home and international students
  • Research Assistant role with additional income (up to approximately 20 hours per week)
  • Funding for travel related to fieldwork and data collection
  • Support for attending conferences and engaging with global research networks

This ensures that candidates can focus fully on their research without financial constraints.

Roles and Responsibilities of PhD Candidates

PhD students under the AHEaD programme will take on multiple roles as researchers, collaborators, and contributors to a broader research ecosystem.

Key Responsibilities

  • Conduct independent doctoral research aligned with AHEaD objectives
  • Support literature reviews and evidence synthesis
  • Participate in data collection and empirical analysis
  • Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams
  • Contribute to policy-relevant research outputs

Candidates are expected to demonstrate initiative, intellectual curiosity, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively.

Eligibility Criteria and Person Specification

The programme seeks highly motivated candidates with strong academic backgrounds and a clear interest in health economics and policy.

Essential Requirements

  • Master’s degree in economics, political economy, or a related field
  • Strong analytical and quantitative skills
  • Experience with economic or policy-relevant datasets
  • Demonstrated interest in diagnostics, health systems, or public policy
  • Excellent academic writing and communication skills
  • Ability to work independently and within teams

Additional Requirements

  • Willingness to reside in the UK for the duration of the programme
  • Availability for international travel (1–2 months at a time)
  • Strong problem-solving and research capabilities

Desirable Qualifications

  • Experience working in African contexts (e.g., Senegal, Ethiopia)
  • French language proficiency
  • Background in econometrics, HTA, or applied economics
  • Knowledge of diagnostic, pharmaceutical, or healthcare markets

Entry Requirements

Applicants must meet LSE’s PhD entry requirements, including:

  • A Master’s degree with a minimum average equivalent to 65%
  • A dissertation mark of at least 65%

Importantly, prior expertise in diagnostics is not required, making this opportunity accessible to candidates from diverse academic and professional backgrounds.

Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide

To apply for the AHEaD PhD studentship, candidates must complete a Formal Application to LSE.

Steps to Apply

  1. Apply to the MPhil/PhD in Health Policy at LSE
  2. Clearly indicate “Economics of Diagnostics: AHEaD” in your application
  3. Submit a research proposal (maximum 1,000 words)

Writing a Strong Research Proposal

The research proposal is a critical component of the application and should demonstrate:

  • Identification of a clear and relevant research gap
  • Justification of the topic’s importance and timeliness
  • Identification of key stakeholders
  • Clear methodology and data sources
  • Potential policy or system-level impact

Applicants should aim for conceptual clarity, methodological rigor, and alignment with micro, meso, or macro-level analysis.

Application Deadline

The final deadline to submit your application is:

April 27, 2026 (5:00 PM)

Late submissions are not accepted, and early preparation is strongly advised.

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

This PhD studentship is not just an academic programme—it is a career-defining opportunity.

Key Advantages

  • Study at one of the world’s leading institutions
  • Work on high-impact, policy-relevant research
  • Gain international exposure and collaboration opportunities
  • Build expertise in a rapidly growing field
  • Contribute to improving healthcare systems across Africa

Contact Information

For informal enquiries, applicants can contact:

Lesong Conteh – l.conteh@lse.ac.uk

Click HERE to learn more about the PhD Studentships in African Health, Economics and Diagnostics at The London School of Economics and Political Science.

Discover more global PhD Scholarship and Research opportunities on OFY.

The post Fully Funded PhD in African Health Economics & Diagnostics at LSE 2026: Complete Guide to the AHEaD Studentship Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Regional Focal Point (APAC) at SDSN Youth 2026: Fully Remote Global Leadership Opportunity in Sustainable Development
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The SDSN Youth Regional Focal Point (RFP) for Asia-Pacific (APAC) is a highly impactful global leadership opportunity designed for passionate young leaders committed to advancing sustainable development. Hosted under the umbrella of the UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network (SDSN), this role places you at the heart of international collaboration, policy engagement, and youth-driven innovation aligned […]

The post Regional Focal Point (APAC) at SDSN Youth 2026: Fully Remote Global Leadership Opportunity in Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The SDSN Youth Regional Focal Point (RFP) for Asia-Pacific (APAC) is a highly impactful global leadership opportunity designed for passionate young leaders committed to advancing sustainable development. Hosted under the umbrella of the UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network (SDSN), this role places you at the heart of international collaboration, policy engagement, and youth-driven innovation aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and the Paris Climate Agreement.

This fully remote position offers an unparalleled platform to work with global stakeholders, coordinate youth-led initiatives, and contribute meaningfully to solving some of the world’s most pressing environmental, social, and economic challenges.

About SDSN: A Global Network for Sustainable Solutions

Established in 2012 under the auspices of the United Nations Secretary-General, SDSN mobilizes scientific and technological expertise worldwide to promote practical solutions for sustainable development. The organization plays a pivotal role in:

  • Advancing the implementation of the SDGs
  • Supporting climate action under the Paris Agreement
  • Promoting integrated approaches to global challenges
  • Facilitating knowledge exchange across countries and sectors

About SDSN Youth: Empowering the Next Generation of Changemakers

Launched in 2015, SDSN Youth is a flagship initiative focused on empowering young people globally to lead sustainable development efforts. Its core mission revolves around:

  • Building youth capacity through education and training
  • Promoting awareness of sustainable development issues
  • Supporting youth-led innovation and advocacy
  • Encouraging collaboration across global networks

Through its extensive network of youth-led organizations, SDSN Youth has become a powerful force in mobilizing young leaders worldwide.

Understanding the Youth Networks Program

The Youth Networks Program is a cornerstone of SDSN Youth’s global strategy. It connects young leaders through national and regional networks, enabling them to:

  1. Develop skills and knowledge related to sustainability
  2. Collaborate with governments, NGOs, and private sector stakeholders
  3. Launch and scale impactful sustainable development initiatives

Each network is coordinated locally by National Coordinators (NCs) and regionally by Regional Focal Points (RFPs), ensuring alignment with global sustainability goals.

Role Overview: Regional Focal Point (APAC)

As a Regional Focal Point, you will serve as the key liaison between SDSN Youth’s global leadership and regional youth networks across the Asia-Pacific region. This role requires strong leadership, communication, and coordination skills to support and guide National Coordinators effectively.

Key Responsibilities

Leadership and Coordination

  • Act as the primary point of contact between SDSN Youth and regional networks
  • Support National Coordinators in project management and strategic planning
  • Foster collaboration and knowledge-sharing across countries

Monitoring and Reporting

  • Track and evaluate activities within regional networks
  • Ensure alignment with SDGs and SDSN Youth objectives
  • Report regional progress to global leadership teams

Communication and Engagement

  • Organize monthly calls with National Coordinators
  • Participate in global and regional meetings
  • Maintain consistent communication across time zones

Strategic Development

  • Identify growth opportunities within the region
  • Support recruitment of new National Coordinators
  • Promote SDSN Youth initiatives to relevant stakeholders

Eligibility Criteria: Who Can Apply?

This opportunity is specifically designed for young leaders who meet the following criteria:

Age Requirement

  • Applicants must be between 18 and 30 years old

Geographic Eligibility

  • Must be based in the Asia-Pacific (APAC) region
  • Must remain in the region for at least 12 months after application

Educational and Professional Background

  • Experience in youth leadership, student organizations, or volunteer work
  • Background in project coordination, event management, or stakeholder engagement is highly desirable

Skills and Competencies

  • Excellent command of the English language (written and spoken)
  • Strong organizational and communication skills
  • Familiarity with tools like Google Workspace
  • Ability to work independently in a remote, multicultural environment

Additional Advantages

  • Prior involvement with SDSN or SDSN Youth networks
  • Connections with youth organizations or sustainability initiatives

Commitment and Working Conditions

Duration

  • Initial term: 12 months
  • Includes a 3-month trial period
  • إمكانية التمديد based on performance

Time Commitment

  • Minimum of 10 hours per week

Work Setup

  • Fully remote (global virtual environment)
  • Requires flexibility to work across multiple time zones

Compensation

  • This is a voluntary (unpaid) position

Benefits: Why You Should Apply

Despite being a voluntary role, the SDSN Youth Regional Focal Point position offers substantial professional and personal development opportunities:

Global Exposure

  • Represent SDSN Youth at international events
  • Engage with global stakeholders across sectors

Skill Development

  • Gain hands-on experience in leadership, coordination, and strategy
  • Access training programs and capacity-building resources

Career Advancement

  • Build a strong personal brand in sustainability
  • Enhance your resume with a globally recognized organization

Networking Opportunities

  • Collaborate with professionals from governments, NGOs, and the private sector
  • Join a vibrant global community of changemakers

Ideal Candidates: Who Should Consider This Role?

This opportunity is best suited for:

  • Young professionals passionate about sustainable development and climate action
  • Emerging leaders seeking international exposure and impact
  • Individuals interested in policy, advocacy, and global collaboration
  • Students or graduates looking to build a career in development, sustainability, or international relations

Application Process

Candidates are encouraged to apply through official platforms such as:

  • Indeed Resume submission
  • LinkedIn application portal

Given the competitive nature of this role and the lack of a fixed deadline, early application is highly recommended.

Apply here

Deadline

Not explicitly specified (applications expected to be reviewed on a rolling basis; early application strongly recommended).

The SDSN Youth Regional Focal Point (APAC) role is more than just a volunteer position—it is a gateway to global leadership in sustainable development. If you are driven by purpose, eager to lead, and committed to shaping a better future, this opportunity offers the perfect platform to make a meaningful impact. Take the next step in your leadership journey and become part of a global movement working toward the 2030 Agenda.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Regional Focal Point (APAC) at SDSN Youth 2026: Fully Remote Global Leadership Opportunity in Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) 2026: Fully Funded Climate Leadership Program for Young Africans
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) 2026 is a premier pan-African capacity-building initiative designed to equip young climate leaders with the knowledge, skills, and confidence to actively participate in global climate governance. As one of the continent’s leading youth-focused climate diplomacy programs, AYNF is dedicated to strengthening African representation in international climate negotiations, particularly within […]

The post African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) 2026: Fully Funded Climate Leadership Program for Young Africans appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) 2026 is a premier pan-African capacity-building initiative designed to equip young climate leaders with the knowledge, skills, and confidence to actively participate in global climate governance. As one of the continent’s leading youth-focused climate diplomacy programs, AYNF is dedicated to strengthening African representation in international climate negotiations, particularly within the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC).

Now in its fifth cohort, the fellowship continues to build a strong and inclusive pipeline of African youth leaders capable of shaping climate policies at national, regional, and global levels. The program is organized by the Green Africa Youth Organization (GAYO), in collaboration with the Environmental Protection Authority (EPA) Ghana and the Climate and Development Knowledge Network (CDKN).

About the African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF)

The AYNF Fellowship was established to address the urgent need for increased African youth participation in climate decision-making processes. Climate negotiations are complex and require both technical expertise and strategic negotiation skills. This fellowship bridges that gap by preparing young Africans to effectively engage in these processes.

Participants gain exposure to:

  • Climate science fundamentals
  • Global climate governance systems
  • Negotiation frameworks and diplomacy
  • Policy analysis and development
  • Leadership and advocacy skills

The program also creates pathways for fellows to engage in key international climate events, including Conference of the Parties (COP) engagements.

Program Structure and Learning Model

The AYNF Fellowship 2026 is delivered in three comprehensive phases, combining online learning, in-person engagement, and long-term mentorship.

Phase 1: Foundational Online Learning

All applicants must begin their journey with a mandatory learning phase through the Ghana Climate Literacy Portal.

Key components include:

  • Completion of the Climate Science 101 course
  • Submission of a certificate of completion during application
  • Possible additional assignments, quizzes, or courses for shortlisted candidates

This phase ensures that all participants have a solid understanding of climate science and foundational concepts before advancing.

Phase 2: Intensive Hybrid Training (Ghana)

Selected fellows will participate in a one-week hybrid training program hosted in Ghana.

This phase includes:

  • Advanced climate negotiation training
  • Policy writing workshops
  • Simulation exercises replicating real-world negotiations
  • Deep exploration of UNFCCC structures and processes
  • Analysis of regional and global climate policy intersections

This immersive experience equips fellows with practical, real-world negotiation and policy skills.

Phase 3: Mentorship and COP31 Engagement

The final phase focuses on applied learning, mentorship, and direct engagement in climate policy processes.

Activities include:

  • Thematic negotiation labs
  • Development of policy briefs
  • One-on-one mentorship and guidance
  • Coordinated engagement before, during, and after COP31
  • Strategic exposure to global climate negotiation platforms

This phase ensures that fellows transition from learners to active contributors in climate governance.

Fellowship Objectives and Impact

The AYNF Fellowship is designed to:

  • Strengthen African youth representation in climate negotiations
  • Build technical expertise in climate science and policy
  • Enhance negotiation and diplomacy skills
  • Foster leadership and advocacy among young Africans
  • Promote inclusive participation in global climate decision-making

The program contributes to shaping a new generation of African climate leaders capable of influencing policies that affect the continent and the world.

Mandatory Pre-Application Requirement

Before submitting an application, all candidates must complete the Climate Science 101 course via the Ghana Climate Literacy Portal.

Steps to follow:

  1. Visit the Ghana Climate Literacy Portal
  2. Click on “Start Learning”
  3. Register or log in
  4. Complete the Climate Science 101 course
  5. Download and save your certificate
  6. Upload the certificate during the application process

Failure to provide this certificate will result in disqualification from the application process.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the AYNF Fellowship 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a citizen of an African country
  • Be between the ages of 15 and 35
  • Demonstrate strong passion for climate justice and diplomacy
  • Have experience or interest in climate policy (national or international)
  • Complete at least one course on the Ghana Climate Literacy Portal
  • Be fully available for all three phases of the program
  • Possess proficiency in English (the language of instruction)

Application Process

Applicants are required to:

  • Complete the Climate Science 101 course
  • Obtain and upload the certificate
  • Fill out the online application form
  • Provide accurate personal and professional information

Shortlisted candidates may be required to complete additional learning tasks before final selection.

Why You Should Apply

The AYNF Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to gain first-hand experience in climate diplomacy while building the skills needed to influence policy and drive change. Participants benefit from high-level training, mentorship, and exposure to global climate processes, positioning them as leaders in the fight against climate change.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) 2026 is:

April 30, 2026, at 11:59 PM GMT

Applicants are strongly encouraged to complete their applications early to avoid last-minute challenges.

Ready to apply?
Fill out the form

Important Notes

  • The program is delivered entirely in English
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted
  • Applicants must be committed to all phases of the fellowship
  • Strong emphasis is placed on authenticity, passion, and commitment

Contact Information

For inquiries, applicants can reach out via email:

aynf@greenafricayouth.org

Click HERE for more information about the African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) 2026.

Discover more global Fellowship and Climate Action opportunities on OFY.

The post African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) 2026: Fully Funded Climate Leadership Program for Young Africans appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GIZ Kenya Jobs and Internships 2026: High-Paying Opportunities in Nairobi with the German Development Agency
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
If you are searching for impactful career opportunities in international development, governance, or sustainability, the latest openings at GIZ Kenya present some of the most competitive and rewarding options in East Africa. Based in Nairobi, these roles span internships and technical advisory positions across critical sectors such as governance, entrepreneurship, migration, and digital trade. About […]

The post GIZ Kenya Jobs and Internships 2026: High-Paying Opportunities in Nairobi with the German Development Agency appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are searching for impactful career opportunities in international development, governance, or sustainability, the latest openings at GIZ Kenya present some of the most competitive and rewarding options in East Africa. Based in Nairobi, these roles span internships and technical advisory positions across critical sectors such as governance, entrepreneurship, migration, and digital trade.

About GIZ Kenya

Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) is a globally recognized organization that delivers international technical cooperation services. Operating in Kenya since 1964, GIZ plays a vital role in:

  • Economic development
  • Green energy and climate initiatives
  • Water resource management
  • Governance and institutional strengthening
  • Food security
  • Peace and security programs

The organization primarily works on behalf of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), while also collaborating with private sector partners to bridge development policy and global trade.

Overview of Available Opportunities

All listed roles are based in Nairobi, Kenya and are currently open for application through Fuzu.

1. Intern – Strengthening Good Governance Programme

  • Category: Internship
  • Location: Nairobi, Kenya

This internship focuses on governance systems, policy implementation, and institutional strengthening. It is ideal for candidates interested in public administration, political science, or international relations.

Deadline: 13 days left

2. Junior Technical Advisor – AWE

  • Category: Job
  • Location: Nairobi, Kenya

A mid-entry level professional role designed for candidates with technical expertise. The position supports program implementation, stakeholder coordination, and advisory services.

Deadline: 12 days left

3. M&E Intern – Programme for Promotion of Self-Employment and Entrepreneurship in Kenya

  • Category: Internship
  • Location: Nairobi, Kenya

This role focuses on Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E), data analysis, and program impact assessment in entrepreneurship development initiatives.

Deadline: 8 days left

4. Intern – Better Migration Management Program

  • Category: Internship
  • Location: Nairobi, Kenya

A specialized internship for candidates interested in migration policy, human rights, and regional cooperation frameworks.

Deadline: 6 days left

Who Can Apply: Eligible Candidates

These opportunities are highly competitive and generally target the following categories of applicants:

1. Educational Background

Applicants are typically expected to have or be pursuing degrees in:

  • International Relations
  • Political Science
  • Economics
  • Development Studies
  • Business Administration
  • Communications
  • Data Science / Statistics (for M&E roles)

2. Professional Level

  • Internships: Suitable for students, recent graduates, or early-career professionals
  • Junior Technical Advisor: Requires prior experience (usually 1–3 years minimum)

3. Geographic Eligibility

  • Primarily open to Kenyan nationals
  • May also be open to East African or African applicants, depending on the role
  • Some positions may consider international candidates, especially those already residing in Kenya

Key Skills and Qualifications Required

Across these roles, GIZ typically looks for candidates with:

  • Strong analytical and research skills
  • Excellent written and verbal communication
  • Project management capabilities
  • Cross-cultural competence and teamwork
  • Interest in international development and policy
  • Digital literacy (especially for M&E and communications roles)

For technical roles:

  • Sector-specific expertise
  • Experience with donor-funded programs
  • Stakeholder engagement experience

Why You Should Apply

Working with GIZ offers unique advantages:

1. International Exposure – You will work in a globally connected environment with international experts and institutions.

2. Career Growth – GIZ roles provide a strong foundation for careers in:

  • United Nations agencies
  • NGOs
  • Government institutions
  • Development consulting

3. Impactful Work – You directly contribute to solving real-world challenges in governance, economic development, and sustainability.

How to Apply

Applications are submitted online via the Fuzu platform. Ensure you prepare:

  • Updated CV
  • Tailored cover letter
  • Academic transcripts (for internships)
  • Relevant certifications

Apply as early as possible to increase your chances, especially for roles closing soon.

Apply here

Deadline

Dates vary, apply as soon as possible.

These opportunities at GIZ Kenya are among the most valuable career openings currently available in the development sector in East Africa. Whether you are a student seeking hands-on experience or a young professional aiming to build a global career, these roles offer a powerful stepping stone.

For more information visit here

For more gl9bal opportunities click here

The post GIZ Kenya Jobs and Internships 2026: High-Paying Opportunities in Nairobi with the German Development Agency appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Rooted Futures Lab Environmental Justice & Technology Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded Remote Opportunity)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Rooted Futures Lab Environmental Justice & Technology (EJIT) Fellowship 2026 is a transformative, project-based summer fellowship designed for emerging leaders working at the intersection of technology, infrastructure, and environmental justice. This highly impactful program offers a unique opportunity to contribute to real-world projects that address critical global challenges while building interdisciplinary expertise. The fellowship […]

The post Rooted Futures Lab Environmental Justice & Technology Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded Remote Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Rooted Futures Lab Environmental Justice & Technology (EJIT) Fellowship 2026 is a transformative, project-based summer fellowship designed for emerging leaders working at the intersection of technology, infrastructure, and environmental justice. This highly impactful program offers a unique opportunity to contribute to real-world projects that address critical global challenges while building interdisciplinary expertise.

The fellowship is structured to empower participants to independently lead projects, collaborate across disciplines, and produce tangible outputs that advance environmental justice in technological systems.

About Rooted Futures Lab

Rooted Futures Lab is an innovative organization committed to addressing the systemic neglect of environmental justice in technology development and application. The organization envisions a future where technological advancements actively support both human and planetary well-being, especially for underserved and marginalized communities.

Through its core pillars of education, research, and action, Rooted Futures Lab works to reshape how technology is designed and deployed globally. The organization draws intellectual inspiration from environmental justice pioneer Dr. Robert Bullard and the concept of “histofuturism,” encouraging critical reflection on past injustices while co-creating equitable futures.

Fellowship Overview

The EJIT Fellowship is designed as a remote, flexible, and interdisciplinary experience that allows fellows to explore complex challenges at the intersection of environmental justice and technology.

Key Details:

  • Duration: June 15 – September 4, 2026 (~12 weeks)
  • Time Commitment: Approximately 20 hours per week (flexible)
  • Location: Fully remote
  • Compensation: USD $3,000 stipend (flexible payment structure)
  • Application Deadline: April 30, 2026
  • Application Review: Rolling basis
  • Notification Timeline: Within 3 weeks after the application deadline

Fellowship Tracks for 2026 Cohort

The 2026 cohort offers four specialized fellowship tracks, each designed to address a distinct dimension of environmental justice in technology.

1. Data Centers & Environmental Justice – Creative Technologist Fellow

This track focuses on uncovering the hidden environmental and social costs of digital infrastructure, particularly data centers. Fellows will use computational and technical methods to investigate these impacts.

Core Responsibilities:

  • Design and execute an independent computational research project
  • Apply techniques such as machine learning, remote sensing, or data scraping
  • Develop datasets, models, or analytical reports
  • Contribute to the Data Center Impact Dashboard
  • Translate technical findings into accessible tools and visualizations

Key Outputs:

  • Research reports, datasets, or computational tools
  • Contributions to open-source platforms and dashboards

This role is ideal for individuals with technical expertise who are interested in applying data-driven approaches to environmental justice challenges.

Learn more HERE.

2. Data Centers & Environmental Justice – Investigative Storyteller Fellow

This track emphasizes storytelling and investigative research into the socio-environmental impacts of data centers.

Core Responsibilities:

  • Conduct an independent investigation into environmental justice issues
  • Produce storytelling outputs (written, visual, audio, or multimedia)
  • Translate complex data into compelling narratives
  • Contribute insights to the Data Center Impact Dashboard

Focus Areas:

  • Land displacement
  • Water consumption
  • Air and noise pollution
  • Rising energy costs

This role is suited for journalists, creatives, and communicators passionate about translating complex systems into accessible stories.

Learn more HERE.

3. Study Hall – Democratic Governance & Collective Systems Fellow

The Study Hall track focuses on building community-driven systems and participatory governance models for environmental justice initiatives.

Core Responsibilities:

  • Research democratic governance systems (e.g., sociocracy)
  • Design tools and frameworks for collective decision-making
  • Support student-led communities in applying these systems
  • Document processes for publications and toolkits

Program Context:

  • Previously engaged 130+ students across North America
  • Expanding into a structured fellowship track

This role is ideal for individuals interested in community organizing, education, and participatory systems design.

Learn more HERE.

4. GLITCH Zine – Editor & Strategist Fellow

The GLITCH Zine Fellowship combines editorial leadership with strategic development of a creative publication platform.

Core Responsibilities:

  • Define editorial vision and theme for the 2026 zine
  • Curate and edit submissions
  • Develop outreach and contributor engagement strategies
  • Oversee production and distribution (print and digital)
  • Build long-term strategy for the zine platform

Strategic Focus:

  • Archiving past issues
  • Building partnerships
  • Expanding audience reach

This role is ideal for individuals with editorial, creative, and strategic experience in publishing or storytelling.

Learn more HERE.

Fellowship Structure and Experience

Across all tracks, fellows will:

  • Work independently while collaborating with peers and staff
  • Participate in weekly check-ins (especially in early stages)
  • Receive mentorship, guidance, and feedback
  • Engage in interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Produce outputs that contribute to real-world impact

Fellows are expected to manage their own timelines, take initiative, and navigate open-ended challenges.

Support and Resources

Participants will receive:

  • Access to Rooted Futures Lab’s interdisciplinary team
  • Technical, editorial, and strategic mentorship
  • Collaboration opportunities with developers, researchers, and storytellers
  • Integration into a global cohort of EJIT fellows
  • Tools and platforms for project development and dissemination

Eligibility Criteria

While specific qualifications vary by track, general requirements include:

  • Strong English communication skills
  • Ability to work independently and manage projects
  • Interest in environmental justice, technology, or related fields
  • Interdisciplinary mindset and openness to collaboration

Technical roles may require experience in areas such as:

  • Machine learning, GIS, or data science
  • Journalism, storytelling, or media production
  • Community organizing or governance systems
  • Editorial and publishing work

Importantly, applicants from non-technical and interdisciplinary backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

Rooted Futures Lab is committed to equity and inclusion. The fellowship does not discriminate based on:

  • Gender or gender identity
  • Sexual orientation
  • Race or ethnicity
  • Religion
  • Age or economic background

Applicants from underrepresented and marginalized communities are especially encouraged to apply.

Application Process

To apply, candidates must submit:

  • Personal information (name, email, location, timezone)
  • LinkedIn profile
  • CV (PDF or document format, max 10MB)
  • Responses to application questions

Applications are submitted via an online form.

For inquiries:
Email: info@rootedfutureslab.io
Subject: “EJIT Fellowship Application”

Learn more and apply for the Rooted Futures Lab Environmental Justice & Technology (EJIT) Fellowship HERE.

Important Notes for Applicants

  • Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis
  • Authenticity is highly valued; applicants are encouraged to use their own voice
  • Minor grammatical errors will not affect selection
  • Over-reliance on AI-generated responses is discouraged

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship offers a rare opportunity to:

  • Work on cutting-edge environmental justice issues
  • Gain hands-on experience in interdisciplinary projects
  • Build a global professional network
  • Receive financial support while contributing to meaningful work
  • Develop outputs that influence policy, research, and community practices

Discover more global Fellowship and Internship opportunities on OFY.

The post Rooted Futures Lab Environmental Justice & Technology Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded Remote Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
